item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1098)—Answers for Saturday, June 13
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch onto that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (June 13)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • PUPPET
  • TEACUP
  • CLASSIC
  • TOY
  • MINIATURE
  • CHRISTMAS
  • STANDARD
  • SPOON
  • HIT
  • NEVERENDING
  • PROSTHETIC
  • SAUCER
  • WEST SIDE
  • OLDIE
  • TONG
  • MAKEUP

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Things you’d see used during afternoon tea
  • Green – Songs that never die
  • Blue – Things you’d see used in theatrical performances
  • Purple – Movie titles that also feature the word ‘story’

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, June 13!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, June 13 (#1098)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – SAUCER, SPOON, TEACUP, TONGS
  • Green – CLASSIC, HIT, OLDIE, STANDARD
  • Blue – MAKEUP, MINIATURE, PUPPET, PROSTHETIC
  • Purple – CHRISTMAS, NEVERENDING, TOY, WEST SIDE

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-june-13-1098/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Microsoft Hasn’t Ruled Off Spinning Off XBOX As It’s Own Entity, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
Another new report has shed some light on the future of XBOX.

XBOX is in a time of change. Asha Sharma took over as CEO of the brand following Phil Spencer’s retirement and Sarah Bond’s subsequent resignation. There was a wave of positive momentum in Sharma’s first few months as CEO. She’s been receptive to fan feedback and has openly discussed her vision for the brand’s future.

However, the reality of recent difficulties is beginning to weigh on her as she faces tough decisions. Recently, she revealed that a restructuring is underway and that the brand will lay off employees in July. Now, a new report exposes more of what may lie ahead for XBOX.

A recent report from The Information (via ResetEra) indicates that Microsoft is considering spinning off or restructuring the XBOX division into a wholly owned subsidiary. This move could facilitate an easier potential sale or lead to the creation of a joint venture with external partners. In news related to the gaming side of XBOX, the brand will be moving faster to develop new games from Top franchises, including Halo, Fallout, and Elder Scrolls. The two Bethesda properties are said to be big areas of focus for Sharma

Additionally, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella and Chief Financial Officer Amy Hood have approved of Sharma’s plan to spend more on top-tier games in the coming fiscal year beginning in July, although the budget is not yet locked in and could change. Also, those same executives haven’t rolled out a restructuring of XBOX’s relationship with Microsoft, and that potential restructuring is making gaming’s green brand a more successful business. Those potential ideas include the aforementioned possible spin-off.

Learn more about forthcoming XBOX layoffs here. Learn about the future of XBOX’s console plans at this link.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available. Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/microsoft-hasnt-ruled-off-spinning-off-xbox-as-its-own-entity-its-claimed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fnatic Takeover Bids Confirmed but CEO Says They Aren’t Going Anywhere
item.content:encoded:
Fnatic founder and CEO Sam Mathews has confirmed that the organisation has received acquisition bids.

A report from RFT claims Fnatic has received several offers ranging from minority stakes to a full acquisition, with football clubs amongst the interested parties.

Fnatic is one of the longest-running esports organisations in the world, but has struggled to replicate previous success.

Now, Mathews has moved to reassure Fnatic fans that, despite the bids, the organisation and the brand itself are going nowhere.

Potential Fnatic Takeover

Reports in the British media claimed that Fnatic would be valued at around $100m (£74.7m) in a sale.

However, RFT now claims that the valuation of Fnatic has been significantly lower in the ongoing negotiations.

With various offers on the table, a decision will supposedly be made in the coming weeks.

This year has been a difficult period for the organisation, with Fnatic struggling across multiple top-tier esports titles.

Fnatic’s League of Legends team is looking to Korea for a new player , having failed to even qualify for the LEC Spring Playoffs.

The team is at risk of failing to qualify for the LoL World Championships for the first time in 10 years.

Meanwhile, Fnatic missed out on Valorant Masters London after a disastrous showing in the VCT EMEA Playoffs.

This was particularly heartbreaking for fans of the UK-based organisation as it would have been a homecoming for British star Jake ‘Boaster’ Howlett.

And while Fnatic made its name in Counter-Strike, it is nowhere near the dominant force in CS2.

Fnatic just parted ways with its longest-serving CS player Freddy ‘KRIMZ’ Johansson after a 12 year stint.

Mathews Clarifies Current Fnatic Situation

While rumours swirl, CEO Mathews claims that Fnatic as a brand and entity will remain.

Responding to LEC broadcast talent Laure Valée, Mathews stated:

“We’re not going anywhere… We’re in investment conversations. Nothing is decided, but yes we’ve had acquisition offers.”

One fan with a Fnatic tattoo asked that the organisation doesn’t change their logo, and Mathews claimed “of course” they won’t.

What do you make of Fnatic’s potential takeover? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord. 

Meanwhile, the Valorant Masters London Playoffs schedule is set after Champions 2025 winners NRG’s surpirse exit.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: G2 Esports Didn’t Scrim Fnatic for the Entirety of LEC Spring
item.content:encoded:
G2 Esports did not scrim rivals Fnatic once throughout the LEC Spring Split 2026 on their road to the title.

G2 won their third LEC split in a row, defeating Karmine Corp 3-2 in yet another thrilling five-game series between the two teams.

The League of Legends team’s general manager, Romain Bigeard, revealed G2’s scrim results throughout the split, which revealed some surprising details.

One of which was that G2 did not play any practice games against their oldest rivals, Fnatic.

Fnatic Are in Trouble

Fnatic are continuing to struggle this year, having failed to even qualify for the Playoffs altogether in Spring.

It’s possible that G2 didn’t think Fnatic would give them enough competition in practice.

G2 also didn’t scrim against Team Heretics, Shifters, or SK Gaming, the other three teams that failed to make the Playoffs.

However, G2 did play against tier-two teams Galions and Solary, who impressed against LEC opposition in the Esports World Cup Qualifiers.

In their current form, Fnatic now find themselves at risk of missing out on the LoL World Championships for the first time in 10 years.

Fnatic have attended every LoL Worlds from 2017 onwards and attended the majority of the events before that too.

The team is also in a long trophy drought, having not won a title since Summer 2019.

As a result, Fnatic are apparently looking to replace their toplaner with a Korean pro to try and turn their season around.

G2 Scrims Anger KCorp Fans

Fnatic fans weren’t the only ones upset with G2’s scrimming decisions.

The post from Bigeard revealed that G2 stopped scrimming Karmine Corp four weeks into the split.

This was most likely so that they had an edge over their main title contenders and didn’t make their opposition stronger.

Some KC fans were furious over this, claiming that G2 were holding their region back by not allowing other teams to get stronger.

Do you agree with G2’s decision to dodge KC scrims or is it cowardly? Share your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord. 

G2 have also secured their future results by extending Steven ‘Hans Sama’ Liv’s contract to 2028.

MOBILE APP
